raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The merit system for school employees in California was created in the 1930s to provide fairness in hiring, retention, and promotion.",
    "Over 90 K-12 and Community College districts in California fall under the merit system.",
    "There is a lack of clarity around merit system employees in California law, potentially leading to unfair labor practices.",
    "Permanent employees in California merit system districts may be required to complete a probationary period when changing positions.",
    "The corresponding law for merit system districts regarding promotional probation procedures is silent, leading to ambiguity.",
    "SB 874 aims to provide clarity and equity between merit system and non-merit system procedures.",
    "California is experiencing a severe staffing shortage in its education system, worsened by COVID-19.",
    "SB 874 is intended to help retain the state's workforce by encouraging promotions within merit system K-12 and Community College districts."
  ]
}
```
